title: Server Technology Information Disclosure
description: The server reveals technology information through response headers.
evidence: Server or X-Powered-By headers are present.
riskImpact: Knowing the exact server software and version allows attackers to search for and exploit known vulnerabilities.
explanation: HTTP response headers like Server and X-Powered-By reveal the technology stack. While not a vulnerability itself, it aids reconnaissance.
fixSteps: Remove or obscure the Server header.
Remove the X-Powered-By header entirely.
In Express.js, use helmet or app.disable('x-powered-by').

codeExamples.label: Apache
codeExamples.code:
ServerTokens Prod
ServerSignature Off
Header unset X-Powered-By

codeExamples.label: Express (Node.js)
Deno (Hono)

title: Missing Cache-Control Headers
description: No Cache-Control or Pragma headers are set. Sensitive responses may be cached.
riskImpact: Sensitive data might be stored in browser caches or shared proxy caches.
explanation: Cache-Control headers instruct browsers and CDNs on how to cache responses. For sensitive data, set no-store.
fixSteps: Set 'Cache-Control: no-store' for sensitive responses.
Use proper cache headers for static assets.
Add 'Pragma: no-cache' as a fallback.

title: Missing Network Error Logging (NEL)
description: Neither NEL nor Report-To headers are present for network error monitoring.
evidence: No NEL or Report-To headers found.
riskImpact: Without NEL, you won't be notified of DNS failures, TCP connection issues, or TLS errors that prevent users from reaching your site.
explanation: Network Error Logging (NEL) is a reporting mechanism that lets browsers report network errors to a specified endpoint. Combined with Report-To, it gives visibility into connectivity issues affecting real users.
fixSteps: Add Report-To and NEL headers to your response.
Configure a reporting endpoint to receive and process the error reports.

title: Network Error Logging Not Configured
description: NEL header is not set, so network errors go undetected.
riskImpact: Network errors and failed requests are invisible without NEL.
explanation: NEL allows browsers to report network errors to a specified endpoint.
fixSteps: Configure a NEL header with a reporting endpoint.
Set up a Report-To group first.

title: Report-To Header Missing
description: Report-To header is not configured.
riskImpact: CSP violations and other errors cannot be reported to your server.
explanation: Report-To defines endpoints where browsers send violation and error reports.
fixSteps: Add Report-To header with a JSON reporting group.

title: X-Powered-By Header Exposed
description: X-Powered-By header reveals technology stack.
riskImpact: Attackers can target known vulnerabilities for the disclosed technology.
explanation: This header reveals backend framework or language information.
fixSteps: Remove the X-Powered-By header in your server config.

title: X-AspNet-Version Header Exposed
description: ASP.NET version disclosed via header.
riskImpact: Reveals exact ASP.NET version for targeted attacks.
explanation: This header is added by ASP.NET and discloses the framework version.
fixSteps: Remove X-AspNet-Version in web.config.

title: X-AspNetMvc-Version Header Exposed
description: ASP.NET MVC version disclosed.
riskImpact: Reveals exact MVC framework version.
explanation: This header discloses the ASP.NET MVC version in use.
fixSteps: Remove X-AspNetMvc-Version header.

title: Via Header Reveals Proxy Info
description: Via header discloses proxy information.
riskImpact: Reveals internal infrastructure and proxy chain.
explanation: The Via header is added by proxies, exposing network topology.
fixSteps: Strip Via header at the edge proxy.

title: X-Runtime Header Exposed
description: X-Runtime header exposes request processing time.
riskImpact: Timing information can be used for timing attacks.
explanation: Processing time can reveal information about backend operations.
fixSteps: Remove X-Runtime header in production.

title: X-Request-Id Exposed to Clients
description: Internal request ID is visible to clients.
riskImpact: Request IDs can be used to correlate requests and understand backend architecture.
explanation: While useful for debugging, exposing request IDs provides unnecessary information to attackers.
fixSteps: Consider removing X-Request-Id from public responses.

title: Backend Server Identity Exposed
description: Headers reveal backend server identity.
riskImpact: Reveals specific server hostnames, enabling targeted attacks.
explanation: Headers like X-Backend-Server, X-Served-By expose internal hostnames.
fixSteps: Remove backend identification headers at the load balancer.

title: CDN Cache Age Reveals Architecture
description: Age header exposes CDN caching details.
riskImpact: Reveals CDN usage and caching behavior.
explanation: Age header shows how long content has been cached.
fixSteps: Consider stripping Age header at edge.

title: Debug Header Detected in Production
description: Debug or diagnostic header found in response.
riskImpact: Debug headers reveal internal state, session tokens, and diagnostic data.
explanation: Debug headers left in production expose information useful for attacks.
fixSteps: Remove all debug headers in production deployments.

title: Sensitive Page Cached Publicly
description: Page with sensitive forms has Cache-Control: public.
evidence: Cache-Control: public on page with forms.
riskImpact: Sensitive data may be cached by CDNs and shared caches.
explanation: Pages with password fields or POST forms should use Cache-Control: no-store.
fixSteps: Use Cache-Control: no-store for sensitive pages.

title: CORS Exposes Many Headers
description: Access-Control-Expose-Headers exposes a broad set of headers.
riskImpact: Exposed headers may leak sensitive server information.
explanation: Only expose headers that client JavaScript actually needs.
fixSteps: Restrict exposed headers to the minimum required.

title: CORS Preflight Cache Too Long
description: Access-Control-Max-Age is set very high.
riskImpact: CORS policy changes take very long to take effect for returning users.
explanation: Long max-age means browsers won't re-check CORS policy for extended periods.
fixSteps: Set Access-Control-Max-Age to a reasonable value (e.g., 3600).

title: ETag Leaks Inode Information
description: ETag header contains inode-size-timestamp format.
riskImpact: Inode numbers reveal filesystem details.
explanation: Apache sometimes includes inode numbers in ETags.
fixSteps: Configure FileETag to exclude inode: FileETag MTime Size

title: Server Header Reveals Detailed Version
description: Server header includes specific version numbers.
riskImpact: Exact version numbers help attackers find known CVEs.
explanation: Server headers like 'Apache/2.4.41' reveal exploitable version info.
fixSteps: Configure server to send minimal Server header.
Use ServerTokens Prod in Apache.

title: Page Is Frameable (No Protection)
description: No clickjacking protection headers detected.
riskImpact: Page can be embedded in iframes by malicious sites.
explanation: Without X-Frame-Options or CSP frame-ancestors, clickjacking is possible.
fixSteps: Add X-Frame-Options: DENY or CSP frame-ancestors 'self'.

title: AWS Request ID Exposed
description: AWS request ID headers present.
riskImpact: Confirms AWS infrastructure usage.
explanation: x-amz-request-id reveals AWS backend.
fixSteps: Remove AWS headers at edge/proxy layer.

title: Cloudflare CF-Ray Header
description: Cloudflare CF-Ray header present.
riskImpact: Confirms Cloudflare CDN usage.
explanation: CF-Ray is Cloudflare's request identifier.
fixSteps: Generally harmless, can be removed at edge.

title: Vercel Deployment ID Exposed
description: Vercel deployment ID header present.
riskImpact: Reveals Vercel hosting.
explanation: x-vercel-id identifies Vercel deployments.
fixSteps: Can be hidden via custom headers config.

title: X-Cache Header Reveals Caching
description: X-Cache header reveals caching behavior.
riskImpact: Reveals CDN caching status.
explanation: Hit/miss status helps attackers understand caching.
fixSteps: Remove X-Cache header at edge if concerned.

title: ETag Contains Inode Information
description: ETag appears to contain filesystem inode.
riskImpact: Reveals internal filesystem details.
explanation: Apache's default ETag includes inode-size-timestamp.
fixSteps: Configure FileETag to use only size and timestamp.

title: Server Time Significantly Different
description: Server date differs significantly from client.
riskImpact: May indicate NTP issues, affects time-based security.
explanation: Time skew can break TOTP, certs, and session expiry.
fixSteps: Sync server time with NTP.

title: Missing Document-Policy Header
description: Document-Policy header not set.
riskImpact: Browser may not restrict certain document features.
explanation: Document-Policy controls various document features.
fixSteps: Add Document-Policy header if needed.

title: Missing Origin-Agent-Cluster
description: Origin-Agent-Cluster header not set.
riskImpact: Browser may not isolate origins in separate processes.
explanation: This header requests per-origin process isolation.
fixSteps: Add Origin-Agent-Cluster: ?1 for isolation.

title: DNS Prefetch Disabled
description: X-DNS-Prefetch-Control is set to off.
riskImpact: May slightly impact performance.
explanation: DNS prefetching speeds up navigation.
fixSteps: Consider enabling unless privacy is a concern.

title: Debug flag set via cookie
description: A debug=true cookie flips debug behaviour — easy to forget when staging.
evidence: Async / body / header check for debug-via-cookie.
riskImpact: Avoid debug toggles via cookie; use environment variables instead
explanation: A debug=true cookie flips debug behaviour — easy to forget when staging.
fixSteps: Avoid debug toggles via cookie; use environment variables instead

title: Set-Cookie header > 4KB
description: Browsers drop cookies larger than ~4KB.
evidence: Async / body / header check for cookie-too-large.
riskImpact: Store less data in cookies; use server-side session storage
explanation: Browsers drop cookies larger than ~4KB.
fixSteps: Store less data in cookies; use server-side session storage

title: Vary Header Missing on Compressed Responses
description: When serving different content based on Accept-Encoding, the Vary header is required to avoid cache poisoning.
evidence: Async / body / header check for vary-header-missing.
riskImpact: Add Vary: Accept-Encoding to all compressed responses
explanation: When serving different content based on Accept-Encoding, the Vary header is required to avoid cache poisoning.
fixSteps: Add Vary: Accept-Encoding to all compressed responses

title: Vary: User-Agent missing on responsive pages
description: Pages that return different content based on User-Agent should include Vary: User-Agent.
evidence: Async / body / header check for vary-header-missing-user-agent.
riskImpact: Add Vary: User-Agent when serving mobile/desktop variants
explanation: Pages that return different content based on User-Agent should include Vary: User-Agent.
fixSteps: Add Vary: User-Agent when serving mobile/desktop variants

title: Vary: Cookie missing on auth-gated content
description: Pages that change based on session cookies must include Vary: Cookie to avoid serving one user's content to another.
evidence: Async / body / header check for vary-header-cookie.
riskImpact: Add Vary: Cookie to all auth-dependent responses
explanation: Pages that change based on session cookies must include Vary: Cookie to avoid serving one user's content to another.
fixSteps: Add Vary: Cookie to all auth-dependent responses

title: Transfer-Encoding: chunked in use
description: Transfer-Encoding: chunked is fine, but worth tracking.
evidence: Async / body / header check for transfer-encoding-chunked.
riskImpact: Prefer Content-Length where possible for cacheability
explanation: Transfer-Encoding: chunked is fine, but worth tracking.
fixSteps: Prefer Content-Length where possible for cacheability

title: Server-Timing exposed publicly
description: Server-Timing without Timing-Allow-Origin: * is browser-restricted, but the value is still logged server-side.
evidence: Async / body / header check for server-timing-allow-origin-public.
riskImpact: Drop Server-Timing for non-debug builds
explanation: Server-Timing without Timing-Allow-Origin: * is browser-restricted, but the value is still logged server-side.
fixSteps: Drop Server-Timing for non-debug builds

title: Content-Disposition: inline for downloadable content
description: PDFs, images, etc. served as inline can be exfiltrated by iframes.
evidence: Async / body / header check for content-disposition-inline.
riskImpact: Add Content-Disposition: attachment; filename=... for sensitive downloads
explanation: PDFs, images, etc. served as inline can be exfiltrated by iframes.
fixSteps: Add Content-Disposition: attachment; filename=... for sensitive downloads

title: X-XSS-Protection set to '1; mode=block'
description: X-XSS-Protection is deprecated and the Chrome auditor has been removed. Modern browsers ignore this header.
evidence: Async / body / header check for x-xss-protection-block.
riskImpact: Remove X-XSS-Protection; rely on CSP instead
explanation: X-XSS-Protection is deprecated and the Chrome auditor has been removed. Modern browsers ignore this header.
fixSteps: Remove X-XSS-Protection; rely on CSP instead

title: Server-Timing exposes cache timings
description: Server-Timing values like cache;dur=12 or edge;dur=4 expose internal cache hit/miss timing to the client.
evidence: Async / body / header check for server-timing-cache-timings.
riskImpact: Drop cache-specific Server-Timing entries outside of debug builds
explanation: Server-Timing values like cache;dur=12 or edge;dur=4 expose internal cache hit/miss timing to the client.
fixSteps: Drop cache-specific Server-Timing entries outside of debug builds

title: Vary: Cookie set on static resource
description: A static asset (image, JS, CSS) served with Vary: Cookie forces every cache to store one copy per Cookie value, defeating caching.
evidence: Async / body / header check for vary-cookie-on-static-resource.
riskImpact: Drop Vary: Cookie from responses on static asset paths
explanation: A static asset (image, JS, CSS) served with Vary: Cookie forces every cache to store one copy per Cookie value, defeating caching.
fixSteps: Drop Vary: Cookie from responses on static asset paths

title: CORS endpoint missing Vary: Origin
description: An endpoint that reflects Access-Control-Allow-Origin per request must also include Vary: Origin so caches don't serve one origin's response to another.
evidence: Async / body / header check for vary-origin-missing-cors.
riskImpact: Add Vary: Origin alongside dynamic Access-Control-Allow-Origin
explanation: An endpoint that reflects Access-Control-Allow-Origin per request must also include Vary: Origin so caches don't serve one origin's response to another.
fixSteps: Add Vary: Origin alongside dynamic Access-Control-Allow-Origin

title: X-Cache-Status reveals Cloudflare caching
description: Cloudflare's X-Cache-Status header reveals HIT/MISS/EXPIRED status and exposes CDN behaviour.
evidence: Async / body / header check for x-cache-status-cloudflare.
riskImpact: Strip X-Cache-Status at the origin if you don't want cache status exposed
explanation: Cloudflare's X-Cache-Status header reveals HIT/MISS/EXPIRED status and exposes CDN behaviour.
fixSteps: Strip X-Cache-Status at the origin if you don't want cache status exposed

title: X-Amz-Cf-Id CloudFront request ID exposed
description: CloudFront's X-Amz-Cf-Id header reveals an AWS CloudFront request identifier on every response.
evidence: Async / body / header check for x-amz-cf-id.
riskImpact: Strip X-Amz-Cf-Id at the edge via Lambda@Edge if you don't want CloudFront request IDs leaked
explanation: CloudFront's X-Amz-Cf-Id header reveals an AWS CloudFront request identifier on every response.
fixSteps: Strip X-Amz-Cf-Id at the edge via Lambda@Edge if you don't want CloudFront request IDs leaked

title: X-Vercel-Cache reveals Vercel edge caching
description: Vercel's X-Vercel-Cache header exposes HIT/MISS/BYPASS status on every response.
evidence: Async / body / header check for x-vercel-cache.
riskImpact: Drop X-Vercel-Cache at the edge or set Cache-Control: private to avoid leaking cache status
explanation: Vercel's X-Vercel-Cache header exposes HIT/MISS/BYPASS status on every response.
fixSteps: Drop X-Vercel-Cache at the edge or set Cache-Control: private to avoid leaking cache status

title: X-Nextjs-Cache reveals Next.js ISR cache
description: Next.js sets X-Nextjs-Cache: HIT | MISS | STALE | BYPASS to indicate ISR/SSG cache state. Helpful for debugging, leaks architecture detail.
evidence: Async / body / header check for x-nextjs-cache.
riskImpact: Remove the header in next.config.js or behind an env flag for production
explanation: Next.js sets X-Nextjs-Cache: HIT | MISS | STALE | BYPASS to indicate ISR/SSG cache state. Helpful for debugging, leaks architecture detail.
fixSteps: Remove the header in next.config.js or behind an env flag for production

title: X-Netlify-Cache exposes Netlify CDN cache state
description: Netlify's X-Netlify-Cache header exposes HIT/MISS/PASS/REVALIDATE status on responses.
evidence: Async / body / header check for x-netlify-cache.
riskImpact: Strip X-Netlify-Cache via _headers if you don't want CDN cache state exposed
explanation: Netlify's X-Netlify-Cache header exposes HIT/MISS/PASS/REVALIDATE status on responses.
fixSteps: Strip X-Netlify-Cache via _headers if you don't want CDN cache state exposed

title: X-Cache-Hits exposes repeated cache hits
description: Some CDNs (Cloudflare, Fastly) include X-Cache-Hits to indicate how many times an asset was served from cache.
evidence: Async / body / header check for x-cache-hits.
riskImpact: Strip X-Cache-Hits if you don't want cache-usage patterns exposed
explanation: Some CDNs (Cloudflare, Fastly) include X-Cache-Hits to indicate how many times an asset was served from cache.
fixSteps: Strip X-Cache-Hits if you don't want cache-usage patterns exposed

title: RateLimit-Policy header missing
description: The draft RateLimit fields (RateLimit-Limit, RateLimit-Remaining, RateLimit-Reset) require a RateLimit-Policy header for clients to interpret windowing rules.
evidence: Async / body / header check for ratelimit-policy-missing.
riskImpact: Add RateLimit-Policy alongside RateLimit-Limit so clients know the limit window and quota
explanation: The draft RateLimit fields (RateLimit-Limit, RateLimit-Remaining, RateLimit-Reset) require a RateLimit-Policy header for clients to interpret windowing rules.
fixSteps: Add RateLimit-Policy alongside RateLimit-Limit so clients know the limit window and quota
